name: Joe MacDonald
heroAlt: Close detail of the wall of names at the Canadian National Vimy Memorial
heroIntro: A battlefield historian who has spent his life carrying the story of Canada's First World War — and who will spend your tour helping you carry a piece of it home.
portraitAlt: Preserved trenches and mine craters on Vimy Ridge, where Joe leads much of his research and tours
credentials: Specialist in the Canadian experience of the First World War on the Western Front
Deep, ground-level familiarity with Vimy Ridge and its battlefields, "largely hidden in plain sight"
Recognised by fellow battlefield guides as one of the best in the business
Trusted by serving and retired Canadian Armed Forces personnel, including a Brigadier General (Retd.)
Personally researches each guest's family history on request, at no extra charge
storyParagraphs: Joe MacDonald has carried a passion for military history since childhood — carrying on that early fascination into a specialisation in the Canadian experience of the First World War. He is, in the words of guests and fellow guides alike, "recognized as one of the best in the business," known for a rare combination of encyclopaedic knowledge and a natural gift for storytelling.

What sets Joe apart is his familiarity with the ground itself — a battlefield today largely hidden in plain sight. He supplements his extensive knowledge with a deep comprehension of the people who fought there, bringing the past vividly into the present through personal and official testimony.
pullQuote: Joe is passionate and very enthusiastic about imparting his impressive knowledge and understanding of Canadians during the First World War... preserving our Canadian military legacy and heritage.
tailoredHeading: Every tour, tailored to who's in it
tailoredParagraph: Whether you're a first-time visitor with little prior knowledge of the Great War, a serving member of the Canadian Armed Forces, or a family hoping to find a great-grandfather's gravesite, Joe tailors each tour to your interests. Ask him to focus on a specific regiment, a specific soldier, or simply to tell the full and heroic story — he is, as one guest put it, "the person you want to lead you through this remarkable period in history."
